summary: Tight 1987 second set highlighted by a memorable Fire transition, captured on an audience‑derived matrix that sounds muted in the highs.
review: Reviewers rave about the second set—calling it one of the best of ’87 and singling out the fire‑filled transition into “Fire” as unforgettable. The tape is an audience‑style matrix recording; while it preserves a solid low end and Bob Weir’s guitar, the high frequencies are muted and Jerry Garcia’s lead sits low in the mix, making the sound quality only fair.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (tight band, standout Fire jam) offset by noticeable recording deficiencies (muted highs, buried lead guitar), leading to an average‑plus overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience‑derived matrix with muted high end; Jerry's guitar low in the mix; low end and Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar clearer; some listeners note it sounds like a multi‑generation soundboard mixed with audience bleed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set described as exceptionally tight and one of the year's best, with seamless transitions that keep momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone present but buried in the mix, lacking volume compared to other versions.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar and low‑end presence come through better than on comparable recordings.
